title: GenAssist: Interactive Prompt-Driven XR Program Generation
summary: GenAssist is a system that generates interactive Extended Reality (XR) programs from natural language prompts. It employs a closed-loop feedback mechanism using virtual cameras to iteratively evaluate and refine generated code, similar to human debugging. The system integrates Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) to ground a large language model in platform-specific API documentation and example code, enabling real-time, hot-pluggable XR program creation. GenAssist supports object placement, animations, and user interactions. A correction LLM uses scene renderings, 3D coordinates, and code to fix errors. Evaluated on a diverse benchmark, it achieves higher accuracy and lower latency (24 seconds) than baselines, making XR development more accessible.
author: Sruti S Srinidhi, Akul Singh, Edward Lu, Anthony Rowe
